entries.definition:
b-k-y to wail, to cry, to bemoan, to lament; to cause to cry; to
pretend to cry. Of this root, three forms occur seven times in the
Qurʾan: بَكَى bakā five times; أَبْكَى ʾabkā once and بُكِيّ bukiyy once.

بَكَى bakā i [v. intrans. with prep. على] to weep, to wail (9:82)
فَلْيَضْحَكُوا قَلِيلًا وَلْيَبْكُوا كَثِيرًا so let them laugh a little; they will weep a
lot; *(44:29) فَمَا بَكَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّمَاءُ وَالْأَرْضُ they were lamented by no
one [lit. neither the heavens nor earth shed a tear for them].

أَبْكَى ʾabkā [v. IV, trans.] to cause to weep, to bring/cause
unhappiness (53:43) وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ أَضْحَكَ وَأَبْكَى and He it is who makes one
laugh, and makes one weep.

بُكِيّ bukiyy [pl. of quasi-act. part. بَاكٍ bākī] weeping (19:58)
خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا they fell down prostrate and weeping.

َل

بَل bal particle denoting turning away from or dismissing what precedes it and retrieving or confirming what follows; it occurs 127 times in the Qur’an. Because بَل is used in argumentative contexts in which ideas are subtly compared and set in contrast the exact meaning of particular occurrences of بَل are often difficult to pinpoint. Broadly speaking, however, بَل functions in the Qur’an in three ways: I [negating or rejecting what precedes and confirming what follows] ‘not that but this’, ‘on the contrary’ (21:62–3) قالوا ءأنت فعلت هذا بآلهتنا ياإبرهيم قال بل فعله كبيرهم هذا they said, ‘Was it you, Abraham, who did this to our gods?’, he said, ‘No, [I did not do it] but this, their chief, did it’ II [setting into contrast or comparing between what precedes and what follows] ‘… but’, ‘… indeed’, ‘… alas’, (87:14–16) قد أفلح من تزكى وذكر اسم ربه فصلى بل تؤثرون الحياة الدنيا the ones who triumph are the ones who purify themselves, [who] remember the name of their Lord and pray, but [there you are!], you [people] prefer the life of this world III [affirming what precedes and adding to it further] ‘… not only that but also …’ (21:5) بل قالوا أضغث أحلم بل افتراه بل هو شاعر فليأتنا بآية كما أرسل الأولون indeed they say, ‘[These are but] muddled dreams; rather he has invented it; rather he is a poet, let him bring us a sign like that previous messengers were sent.’
